figures.caption: Figure 3: Comparison of the centrally computed SNE (µ∗, π∗) and the approximate equilibrium (µ̄I , π̄I) returned by FP-DQN, averaged across 10 seeds. Top: expected bid as a function of karma balance for each urgency level. Bottom: stationary karma distribution for each urgency level.
Figure 7: Comparison of the centrally computed equilibrium policy π∗ and the learned policies by setting Ns = 1M and N = 1000, respectively. The results are averaged across 10 seeds.

Figure 9: Comparison of the centrally computed SNE (µ∗, π∗) and the approximate equilibrium (µ̄I , π̄I) returned by FP-DQN at convergence, averaged across 10 seeds, for the first Karma economy instance. Top: expected bid as a function of karma balance for each urgency level. Bottom: stationary karma distribution for each urgency level.
Figure 4: Comparison of the centrally computed equilibrium policy π∗ and the learned policies by setting Ns = 50k and N = 100, respectively. The results are averaged across 10 seeds.
Figure 5: Comparison of the centrally computed equilibrium policy π∗ and the learned policies by setting Ns = 100k and N = 200, respectively. The results are averaged across 10 seeds.
Figure 6: Comparison of the centrally computed equilibrium policy π∗ and the learned policies by setting Ns = 500k and N = 500, respectively. The results are averaged across 10 seeds.
